question: [File: 'File Name: rest/server.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: responseCacheCheckHandler] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `cacheKeyGenerator` using `req.originalUrl` (which includes query parameters) to enumerate cached transaction queries by brute-forcing common query patterns, then use cache hits (fast responses) vs misses (slow DB responses) as a timing oracle to discover which transaction queries have been recently executed by other users?
